remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Governance as acceleration: data proves it’s not a speed bump
remarks.content: For years, the enterprise AI governance conversation has been shaped by a quiet assumption: Governance is the thing that slows AI down. This may look like the review cycle that delays the launch, committee that questions the model, or even documentation requirement that sits between a wo
remarks.contentTranslation: 多年来，企业AI治理对话一直被一个安静的假设所塑造：治理是减缓AI的因素。这可能看起来像延迟发布的审核周期、质疑模型的委员会，甚至是WO之间的文档要求
remarks.translation: 治理作为加速：数据证明它不是减速带

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Generative AI governance framework: building responsible AI systems
remarks.content: Generative AI is no longer a pilot that leaders can observe from a distance. It is in contact centers, developer workflows, reporting layers, and autonomous agents acting on behalf of the business. As BARC's research on modernizing governance for the era of agentic AI shows, the stakes r
remarks.contentTranslation: 生成式AI不再是领导者可以远距离观察的试点。它存在于联系中心、开发人员工作流、报告层和代表业务行事的自主Agent中。正如BARC关于智能AI时代治理现代化的研究所表明的那样，
remarks.translation: 生成式AI治理框架：构建负责任的AI系统

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Sovereignty isn't where your AI runs. It's whether it answers to you.
remarks.content: Last week, a government order took the world's best AI model offline in hours. No one deleted it and no server failed. A single directive put the most capable model on the market out of reach for everyone not a citizen of one country.
remarks.contentTranslation: 上周，一项政府命令在数小时内让世界上最好的AI模型下线。没有人将其删除，也没有服务器发生故障。单一指令使市场上最有能力的模型对每个人不是一个国家的公民来说都是遥不可及的。
remarks.translation: 主权并不是你的AI运行的地方。问题在于它是否对你有所回应。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: What is an AI orchestration layer? Architecture, benefits, and enterprise use cases
remarks.content: AI investments are multiplying, but coordination between models, agents, and data pipelines remains an afterthought in most enterprises. The result is predictable: duplicated workflows, inconsistent outputs, ungoverned decisions, and AI initiatives that never graduate from pilot to produ
remarks.contentTranslation: AI投资正在成倍增加，但在大多数企业中，模型、Agent和数据管道之间的协调仍然是事后的想法。结果是可预测的：重复的工作流、不一致的输出、不受控制的决策以及从试点到生产的AI计划
remarks.translation: 什么是AI编排层？架构、优势和企业用例

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: AI explainability in enterprise AI: methods, tools, and why it matters for trustworthy models and agents
remarks.content: AI models, generative AI (GenAI) applications, and agents are making decisions that affect credit scores, patient care, and hiring outcomes. Enterprises deploying these systems are accumulating regulatory and reputational risk proportional to the number of decisions that cannot be explai
remarks.contentTranslation: AI模型、生成式AI(GenAI)应用程序和Agent商正在做出影响信用评分、患者护理和招聘结果的决策。部署这些系统的企业正在积累与无法解释的决策数量成比例的监管和声誉风险
remarks.translation: 企业AI中的AI可解释性：方法、工具及其对可信模型和Agent的重要性

remarks.text: Agentic AI tools in 2026: what to look for when choosing an enterprise-grade solution
remarks.content: Most vendors claim their agentic AI platform is enterprise-grade. In a market where the term has lost almost all meaning, the real test is not the demo. It is what happens after, when agents meet real data, real governance requirements, and the organizational complexity that no sandbox c
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数供应商声称他们的AgentAI平台是企业级的。在这个术语几乎失去所有意义的市场中，真正的考验不是演示。当座席满足真实数据、真实治理要求以及没有沙箱的组织复杂性时，

remarks.name: Amanda Askell
remarks.role: Philosopher & ethicist trying to make AI be good AnthropicAI.
remarks.text: I had chronic pain for most of my life until a doctor did an MRI of the pain source and found a congenital condition that was then fixed with surgery. Now I'm wondering if I had 30+ years of pain because doctors worried I was too stupid to be in the presence of scan results.
remarks.translation: 我一生中大部分时间都有慢性疼痛，直到医生对疼痛源进行了MRI检查，发现了一种先天性疾病，然后通过手术固定下来。现在我想知道我是否经历了30多年的疼痛，因为医生担心我在扫描结果面前太愚蠢了。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
I used to be a die-hard Claude Code user.

Codex has won me over because:

→ GPT-5.5 is excellent
→ Fast mode + generous limits = more reps
→ Little touches like steering, auto remote control on phone, etc

But most of all Codex's browser and computer use capabilities are simply goated. I built so many workflows relying on those two things alone instead of hunting for APIs.

I still use Claude Code too. The app seems to be getting better and the design and frontend capability of Opus is still much better than GPT. Whenever Fable comes back that's another reason to go back.

Honestly, I hope these two compete forever and other players (Cursor/Grok, Gemini, etc) all stay competitive.

This way the builder keeps winning 🙂
remarks.translation:
我曾经是一个顽固的Claude Code用户。Codex赢得了我的青睐，因为： → GPT-5.5是出色的→快速模式+慷慨的限制=更多的代表→小小的触摸，如转向，手机上的自动远程控制等，但大多数Codex的浏览器和计算机使用功能都简单地得到了提升。我构建了如此多的工作流，仅仅依靠这两件事，而不是寻找API。我还在使用Claude Code。
应用程序似乎越来越好， Opus的设计和前端功能仍然比GPT好得多。每当寓言回来时，这是另一个回去的理由。老实说，我希望这两个人永远竞争，其他玩家（ Cursor/Grok、Gemini等）都能保持竞争力。通过这种方式，建筑商不断获胜 🙂
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
The next hot programming language is… markdown.

A minimal eve agent:

📂 𝚊𝚐𝚎𝚗𝚝/
  📄 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗𝚜.𝚖𝚍
  📂 𝚜𝚔𝚒𝚕𝚕𝚜/
     📄 𝚢𝚘𝚞𝚛-𝚎𝚡𝚙𝚎𝚛𝚝𝚒𝚜𝚎.𝚖𝚍

Deployable in one command: 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕.

It’s the most accessible programming has ever been. And likely will ever be, at least for the generation of software fully defined and controlled by us humans.

(As a fun fact, one of the initial prototypes for eve was codenamed 𝚕𝚊𝚜𝚝 by @timolins, both in homage to ‘@nextjs for agents’ but also in recognition of how enduring eve’s design feels to us.)
remarks.translation:
下一个热门编程语言是… markdown。最小EVE📂试剂： μ μ μ 📄 μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ 📂 μ μ μ μ μ μ μ 📄 μ μ μ μ μ μ μ - μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ μ。可以在一个命令中部署：这是有史以来最容易使用的编程。而且很可能永远都是这样，至少对于完全由我们人类定义和控制的软件的生成来说是这样。
（作为一个有趣的事实， EVE的初始原型之一被@ timolins代号为“” ，既是为了向“@ nextjs for agents”致敬，也是为了表彰EVE的设计对我们的持久性。）
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Amanda Askell
remarks.role: Philosopher & ethicist trying to make AI be good AnthropicAI.
remarks.text: A counter to this is "yes but people *don't* ignore it". But not ignoring things on scans is our norm because, until recently, we only did scans if there was a clear need. If we move to a scan-more-often paradigm, the norms of what we do with that information will surely adjust.
remarks.translation: 与此相反的是“是的，但人们*不会*忽视它”。但是，不忽视扫描内容是我们的常态，因为直到最近，我们只有在明确需要的情况下才进行扫描。如果我们转向更常见的扫描范式，我们如何处理这些信息的规范肯定会调整。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Amanda Askell
remarks.role: Philosopher & ethicist trying to make AI be good AnthropicAI.
remarks.text: The view that we shouldn't do more medical scans because incidental findings cause a lot of harm doesn't sit well with me. It seems like the issue it points to isn't the scan but the response to it. If you see something on a scan but have no other symptoms, you could ignore it.
remarks.translation: 认为我们不应该进行更多的医疗扫描，因为偶然发现会造成很多伤害的观点并不适合我。它所指出的问题似乎不是扫描，而是对扫描的反应。如果您在扫描中看到某些东西，但没有其他症状，您可以忽略它。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The main variable in getting success with agents is whether you can get the agent the context it needs to do its work; and a major factor in that is if you can create a shared working area for that agent that a human can understand as well. 

This is one of the reasons why agents using file systems is such a big deal. It creates a unified system that both the person and the agent can work within to pass around data. 

“What they need is a working set: plans, notes, task lists, policies, drafts, summaries, logs, corrections,  decisions, etc. For that layer, a filesystem-shaped interface tends to be more legible to both the model and the humans supervising it.”

It turns out giving agents access to the systems we already know how to use, but in a way that is best optimized for them, is the perfect primitive for agents to work.
remarks.translation:
与客服代表取得成功的主要变量是，您是否可以让客服代表了解完成工作所需的上下文；其中一个主要因素是，您是否可以为该客服代表创建一个人类也能理解的共享工作区域。这就是Agent使用文件系统如此重要的原因之一。它创建了一个统一的系统，个人和Agent都可以在其中工作以传递数据。
“他们需要的是一个工作集：计划、注释、任务列表、政策、草案、摘要、日志、更正、决定等。对于该层，文件系统形状的界面往往对模型和监督它的人来说都更清晰。“事实证明，让Agent访问我们已经知道如何使用的系统，但以最适合他们的方式，是Agent工作的完美原始方式。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Agents are motivating so many healthy software habits. Open APIs, documentation (skills), tests (evals), Unix (CLIs), payment & commerce protocols, even wide 𝙰𝚌𝚌𝚎𝚙𝚝 use (markdown/json/html).

The original vision of the WWW coming to life before our eyes.
remarks.translation: Agent正在激励许多健康的软件习惯。开放式API、文档（技能）、测试（ EVALS ）、Unix （ CLI ）、支付和商务协议，甚至广泛使用（ markdown/json/html ）。WWW最初的愿景在我们眼前栩栩如生。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。
